title: Working Tree, Index, Commits, and Atomic History · Command Recall
summary: Turn a mixed set of feature, formatting, and accidental changes into reviewable commits without losing work. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an atomic commit construction workbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.
Read branch, staged, unstaged, untracked, rename, conflict, and ahead/behind status without treating short output as context-free.
Select working-tree, staged, commit-to-commit, path-limited, word, and summary diffs from the review question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff. Competency probe: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.

Inspect the Atomic Commit Message trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.

content.text: For Atomic Commit Message, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Atomic Commit Message precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A large commit is acceptable when its message lists every changed file.

stimulus.title: Atomic Commit Message case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff. Competency probe: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A large commit is acceptable when its message lists every changed file.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data. Artifact under review: an atomic commit construction workbook.

options.text: The Atomic Commit Message record says an atomic commit construction workbook looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Atomic Commit Message without its stated preconditions, omit the an atomic commit construction workbook trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Atomic Commit Message for an atomic commit construction workbook.
The record names the assumption, applies Atomic Commit Message, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Atomic Commit Message: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff. Competency probe: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.
steps.why: Retrieving Atomic Commit Message through the concrete an atomic commit construction workbook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.
